destinations.evidence: SPC verification reads and caches only executor keys needed by settlement jobs and rejects empty keys before signature verification.
The registry reader used by block verification bounds dynamic public-key reads and returns an error for keys longer than MAX_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN.
The port retains the bounded public-key length constant used during registry storage reads.

equivalence_argument: Although the reader crate is named ritual-tee-registry-reader in the port, SPC verification obtains parent-state executor public keys through it, rejects empty keys locally, and inherits the MAX_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN rejection from read_public_key_from_slot.
notes: Mapped to the renamed TEE registry reader and its block-verification call site.
rationale: The merge replaces ritual_executor_registry_reader usage with verifier-local reads for deduplicated executor addresses, adds MAX_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN=128, and explicitly errors on empty or oversized public keys before verifying SPC signatures. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: SPC block verification reads only the needed executor public keys from ExecutorRegistry storage at the parent state, rejects empty keys, and rejects public keys longer than MAX_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN bytes.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create parent-state registry storage for two SPC executor addresses and assert only those keys are read and used; then set one key empty or length 129 and assert block verification returns SpcExecutorRegistryReadFailed.
summary: SPC verification reads needed parent-state executor keys and rejects empty or oversized registry public keys.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create two SPC jobs sharing one executor and one with a second executor; assert only those keys are read/cached, then set one key empty and another length 129 and assert verification rejects.
title: Executor public keys are bounded registry reads
